observations.recommendation:
Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as this activity is very important in checking actual existence of the assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial responsibilities. 
 Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter.
 Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management 2020 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
 Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all
PPE, as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the
assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as
pinpoint custodial responsibilities;
 Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on
PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor
immediately thereafter.
 Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger
Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and
likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete
and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that:
 The agency head should request or even demand from the bank the early
issuance of bank statements especially the year-end. Thereafter, require the
municipal accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements
(BRS) on its depository accounts pursuant to the provisions of Section 3.2 of
COA Cir. No. 96 -011 dated Oct. 12 1996.
 Likewise, the BRS be submitted on time as required under Sec. 74 of PD
1445.

Recommendation
 Management should strictly observe adherence to the existing policies on
Disbursement System that involves granting, utilization, and liquidation of
cash advance under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002;
 Liquidate cash advance within the prescribed period and submit report
thereon to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the journals and books
of account, and subsequently turn over to the auditor not later than the fifth
day of the ensuing month for necessary examination and audit.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the group tasked to function as the Municipality’s
LDRRMO be required to prepare a report on the utilization of LDRRMF to be
submitted in accordance with the provisions of paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular
No. 2012-002.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the
proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the
implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct
advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women,
discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater
role for men in raising a family.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Engineer must request for inspection of various
Infrastructure projects and enhance cooperation and support to the COA
Inspectorate Team from the Regional Technical and Information Technology
Services in the inspection of said project by providing it with the complete project
requirements and guiding the COA engineers to the project sites.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management should direct the procuring department to notify
the Audit Team Leader or any of its representatives on any procurement of
supplies, materials and office equipment consummated for the agency’s
operational purposes during the procuring period within 24 hours after
acceptance thereof.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the municipal accountant and
municipal treasurer to observe strictly the schedule of rendition of accounts, in
accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006, so that the
Auditor could perform timely audit of the financial transactions of the agency.

We recommended that:
 The management should avoid the existing practice of paying the expenses
through reimbursements, all procurements should be a direct payment to the
supplier unless it is impracticable to do so; and
 Establish a Petty Cash fund to defray various miscellaneous supplies and materials
in accordance with the provisions of COA Cir. No. 90-331, as amended.

Recommendation:
We recommend the concerned accountable officers particularly the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the schedule for
rendition of accounts as required for under Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445
otherwise known as the “State Audit Code of the Philippines” and likewise
Section 6.03 of COA Cir. No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials of the Municipal Government of Hadji Panglima Tahil to support all claims with sufficient and required documentations, particularly, on the utilization of the COVID 19 appropriated funds in procurement of medical related goods and services. Likewise, management must strictly observe laws, rules and regulations as provided for in Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on proper and sufficient required documentations and Section 52.1 of RA 9184 on Shopping as an alternative mode of procurement.

We recommend that officials concerned and its local Bids and Award Committee personnel to regularly and to adhere to proper internal processes by providing the resident auditor in advance with copies of the Purchase Orders and within 24 hours after acceptance of such procurement inform the assigned audit team leader of the deliveries made.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management 2020 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
The agency head should request or even demand from the bank the early issuance of bank statements especially the year-end. Thereafter, require the municipal accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements (BRS) on its depository accounts pursuant to the provisions of Section 3.2 of COA Cir. No. 96 -011 dated Oct. 12 1996.
Likewise, the BRS be submitted on time as required under Sec. 74 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management 2020 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
Management should strictly observe adherence to the existing policies on Disbursement System that involves granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advance under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002;
Liquidate cash advance within the prescribed period and submit report thereon to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the journals and books of account, and subsequently turn over to the auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month for necessary examination and audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM officer shall faithfully adhere to all the laws and regulation concerning DRRM fund particularly the submission of the annual local disaster risk reduction management investment plan (LDRRMFIP) for 2020 DRRM transaction per COA Circular 2012-002 to support its legality.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management 2019 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a family.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management 2020 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the municipal accountant and municipal treasurer to observe strictly the schedule of rendition of accounts, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006, so that the Auditor could perform timely audit of the financial transactions of the agency.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management 2019 noted the herein Findi audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Municipal Assessor should update the Master List of Real Property Taxpayers to provide the Municipal Treasurer an accurate basis in the preparation of certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. The certified list shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up the RPT and SET Receivables as required under Section 20, NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management 2020 noted the herein Findi audit recommendation.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Government being primarily responsible for the implementation and enforcement of the provision of R.A 9003 within their respective jurisdictions to comply with the laws and regulation stated in Section 12 and Section 16 of the same Act.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a family.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective means; and require the Municipal Engineer to inform our Office within ten (10) days after the award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of the projects/ programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects signboards and /or public notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this Office.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the municipal accountant and municipal treasurer to observe strictly the schedule of rendition of accounts, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006, so that the Auditor could perform timely audit of the financial transactions of the agency.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 noted the herein Findi ng recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all accountable officers prepare and submit the RAAF to the Office of the Treasurer at the end of each month to ensure all accountable forms issued are properly recorded and reported. Also, we recommend that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit the CRAAF to the Office of the Auditor as required under Section 51 of the NGAS, Volume II, for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 noted the herein Findi ng recommendation.

observations.recommendation: a. The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2), P.D. 144 ); b. Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and c. Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a. The agency head should request or even demand from the bank the early
issuance of bank statements especially the year-end. Thereafter, require the
municipal accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements (BRS)
on its depository accounts pursuant to the provisions of Section 3.2 of COA
Cir. No. 96 -011 dated Oct. 12 1996.
b. Likewise, the BRS be submitted on time as required under Sec. 74 of PD 1445.

 Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE,
as this activity is very important in checking actual existence of the assets
and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint
custodial responsibilities. .
 Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count
on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor
immediately thereafter. Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary
Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile
balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical
Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in
the financial statements.

Recommendation
a. Management should strictly observe adherence to the existing policies on
Disbursement System that involves granting, utilization, and liquidation of
cash advance under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002;
b. Liquidate cash advance within the prescribed period and submit report
thereon to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the journals and books
of account, and subsequently turn over to the auditor not later than the fifth
day of the ensuing month for necessary examination and audit.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM officer shall
faithfully adhere to all the laws and regulation concerning DRRM fund
particularly the submission of the annual local disaster risk reduction
management fund investment plan (LDRRMFIP) for 2019 DRRM transaction per
COA Circular 2012-002 to support its legality.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Government being primarily responsible for
the implementation and enforcement of the provision of R.A 9003 within their
respective jurisdictions to comply with the laws and regulation stated in Section
12 and Section 16 of the same Act.

We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the
proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the
implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct
advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women,
discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater
role for men in raising a family.

We recommend that management strictly comply with the relevant provisions of
COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance
through transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective means; and
require the Municipal Engineer to inform our office within ten (10) days after the
award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of the projects/
programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects signboards and /or
public notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this
Office.

We recommended that Management require the municipal accountant and
municipal treasurer to observe strictly the schedule of rendition of accounts, in
accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006, so that the
Auditor could perform timely audit of the financial transactions of the agency.

Recommendations:
We recommend that all accountable officers prepare and submit the RAAF to the
Office of the Treasurer at the end of each month to ensure all accountable forms
issued are properly recorded and reported.

Also, we recommend that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer prepare and
submit the CRAAF to the Office of the Auditor as required under Section 51 of the
NGAS, Volume II, for LGUs.

We recommended that:
a. The management should instruct concerned officials to submit
disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the
deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the
ensuing month. Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the
withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel
until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are
being complied with (section 122(2), P.D. 144 );
b. Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to
maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent
work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and
c. Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and
Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their
transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting
documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the
regulations.

We recommended that Municipal Engineer must request for inspection of various
Infrastructure projects and enhance cooperation and support to the COA
Inspectorate Team from the Regional Technical and Information Technology
Services in the inspection of said project by providing it with the complete project
requirements and guiding the COA engineers to the project sites.

The agency head should request or even demand from the bank the early issuance of bank statements especially the year-end. Thereafter, require the municipal accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements (BRS) on its depository accounts pursuant to the provisions of Section 3.2 of COA Cir. No. 96 -011 dated Oct. 12 1996. Likewise, the BRS be submitted on time as required under Sec. 74 of PD 1445.

Management should strictly observe adherence to the existing policies on Disbursement System that involves granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advance under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002; Liquidate cash advance within the prescribed period and submit report thereon to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the journals and books of account, and subsequently turn over to the auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month for necessary examination and audit.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly observe adherence to the provisions of Section 21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002; (i) the MPDO prepares Annual Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and annual work and financial plan where disbursements of LDRRMF is strictly based; (ii) transfer to a special trust fund the unexpended LDRRMF for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) within the next five year.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive directs the municipal accountant to immediately open a separate bank account solely for the unexpended/unobligated balance at year-end of the LDRRMF, QRF, NDRRMF and other required sources in accordance with the regulations provided for under Par. 5.1.10 and 5.1.12 of COA Cir. No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that management strictly comply with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective means; and require the Municipal Engineer to inform our office within ten (10) days after the award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of the projects/ programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects signboards and /or public notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this Office.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a family.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Plan be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein Part audit recommendation.

Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and c. Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.

No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Part II Finding No. 9

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a. The agency head should request or even demand from the bank the early
issuance of bank statements especially the year-end. Thereafter, require the
municipal accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements (BRS)
on its depository accounts pursuant to the provisions of Section 3.2 of COA
Cir. No. 96 -011 dated Oct. 12 1996.
b. Likewise, the BRS be submitted on time as required under Sec. 74 of PD 1445.

We recommended that:
 Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as
this activity is very important in checking actual existence of the assets and
determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial
responsibilities. .
 Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on
PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor
immediately thereafter. Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary
Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances
with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to
be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial
statements.

Recommendation
a. Management should strictly observe adherence to the existing policies on
Disbursement System that involves granting, utilization, and liquidation of
cash advance under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002;
b. Liquidate cash advance within the prescribed period and submit report
thereon to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the journals and books
of account, and subsequently turn over to the auditor not later than the fifth
day of the ensuing month for necessary examination and audit.

We recommend that Management strictly observe adherence to the provisions of
Section 21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002; (i) the MPDO prepares
Annual Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and annual work
and financial plan where disbursements of LDRRMF is strictly based; (ii)
transfer to a special trust fund the unexpended LDRRMF for the purpose of
supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local
Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) within the next five
year.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive directs the municipal accountant
to immediately open a separate bank account solely for the
unexpended/unobligated balance at year-end of the LDRRMF, QRF, NDRRMF
and other sources in accordance with the regulations and guidelines provided for
under COA Cir. No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that management strictly comply with the relevant provisions of
COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance
through transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective means; and
require the Municipal Engineer to inform our office within ten (10) days after the
award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of the projects/
programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects signboards and /or
public notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this
Office.

We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the
proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the
implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development,
conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women,
discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater
role for men in raising a family.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change
management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste
Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an
arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner
in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of
results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.

We recommended that Management require the municipal accountant and
municipal treasurer to observe strictly the schedule of rendition of accounts, in
accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006, so that the
Auditor could perform timely audit of the financial transactions of the agency.

Management should follow strictly Section 32, Volume 1 of NGAS (LGUs) Manual and require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collection intact with the authorized depository bank to avoid exposing the funds from possible losses through theft or robbery.

It is recommended that Management (i) strictly observe adherence to Section 74 of PD 1445, and Section 310 of RA 7160 and Rule XXXVI, Article 450 of its IRR; (ii) prepare and submit bank reconciliation statements on all funds monthly to the auditor for verification; and, (iii) maintain separate books and depository accounts for General Fund and Trust Fund in order to easily identify and establish the correct balances of each agency fund per cashbooks that shall correspond with the balances of cash in bank per bank reconciliation statements for the audit period.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Part II Finding No. 2

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that accountable officers must strictly adhere to the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any officials or employees unless the previous cash advance is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made. Furthermore, liquidation of cash advances should be made as required by regulation.

We have recommended that the group tasked to function as the Municipality’s LDRRMO be required to prepare a report on the utilization of LDRRMF to be submitted in accordance with the provisions of paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that management strictly comply with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability, at least cost or effective means; and require the Municipal Engineer to inform our office within ten (10) days after the award of the Infrastructure projects on or before the start of the projects/ programs/ activities (PPAs) that the appropriate projects signboards and /or public notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this Office.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Part II Finding No. 7

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a family.

Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial responsibilities. 
Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter.
Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.

The municipal accountant should be required to record all consummated procurement thru the inventory account and properly follow the steps of recording incoming and out-going supplies and materials in a perpetual method of inventory keeping.  This will prevent purchases in bulk be charged to outright expense; and
Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer must maintain Stock Cards as required for in Section 119 of NGAS Manual Vol. I for local government units.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due and surcharges.  Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a. The agency head should request or even demand from the bank the early issuance of bank statements especially the year-end.  Thereafter, require the municipal accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements (BRS) on its depository accounts pursuant to the provisions of Section 3.2 of COA Cir. No. 96 -011 dated Oct. 12 1996.
b. Likewise, the BRS be submitted on time as required under Sec. 74 of PD 1445.

Recommendation:
Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial responsibilities;
Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter; and
Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.

We have recommended that accountable officers must strictly adhere to the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any officials or employees unless the previous cash advance is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made.  Furthermore, liquidation of cash advances should be made as required by regulation.

We have recommended that the group tasked to function as the Municipality’s LDRRMO be required to prepare a report on the utilization of LDRRMF to be submitted in accordance with the provisions of paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the proper disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a family.

We recommended that management observe the general guidelines stipulated in COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies.  This is to promote Good Governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommended that Management require the municipal accountant and municipal treasurer to observe strictly the schedule of rendition of accounts, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006, so that the Auditor could perform timely audit of the financial transactions of the agency.

We recommend that management should ensure that due notification to the Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods and services be made on time pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommended that
The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2), P.D. 1445);
Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and
Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

The agency head should request or even demand from the bank the early     issuance of bank statements especially the year-end.  Thereafter, require the municipal accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements (BRS) on its depository accounts pursuant to the provisions of Section 3.2 of COA Cir. No. 96 -011 dated Oct. 12 1996. 

Likewise, the BRS be submitted on time as required under Sec. 74 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management observe the general guidelines stipulated in COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies.  This is to promote Good Governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommended that, the management must prepare Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in support of disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC, as stated in COA Circular No. 2012-002.  Management must also evaluate the basic needs as against the capability to timely procure and distribute goods or deliver on time, needed social/health services during crisis time.  This effective and efficient delivery of immediate concerns during crisis time can best be addressed following the guidelines and procedures in the adoption of an appropriate disaster preparedness plan incorporating all crisis preparedness and prevention activities subject to the declaration of imminent danger of calamity by the local sanggunian.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the monitoring team should be created to ensure the  proper    disbursement of the GAD Fund, monitor, evaluate and review the implementation of the Municipality’s policy on Gender and Development, conduct advocacy programs promoting partnership between men and women, discouraging gender based violence between them and encouraging a greater role for men in raising a family.

We recommend that copies of Purchased Orders/Contracts and Acceptance     Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required under Section A (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommend that management strictly undertake procurement of commonly-use Goods such as supplies, materials and equipment for the infrastructures and livelihood projects of the Municipality through the Procurement Service (PS)-Department of Budget and Management (DBM) using the Philippine Government Electronic Procurement System (PhilGEPS), in compliance with Section 53 e), IRR-A of RA 9184, except for emergency cases as determined by proper authority and unavailability of items to be procured in the PS-DBM and the amount do not reach the minimum requirement for public bidding, and provided also that the claim for payment shall be supported with approved BAC Resolution recommending to the Head of Procuring Entity (HOPE), that mode of procurement thru shopping shall be allowed,. As such, we also request management to visit PS-DBM Regional Office IX, Zamboanga City for inquiries and/or assistance in order to facilitate undertaking procurement through PS-DBM using PhilGEPS.
observations.managementAction: Management noted the herein audit recommendation.

Efforts had been made by the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer in coming up with the timely submission of their required reports of transactions, paid disbursement vouchers/payrolls for recording, and for examination and audit.
